document.source_title: Russia launches unannounced nuclear exercise, including Belarusian launch sites
document.author: Linus Höller
document.summary: Russia has initiated its largest nuclear exercises in years, mobilizing approximately 65,000 troops and a significant array of military assets. The drills, which include participation from Belarusian forces, are framed as a rehearsal for potential nuclear responses to aggression. This unannounced exercise comes at a time of heightened tensions and is seen as a strategic move to influence NATO and showcase military readiness.
key_takeaways: Russia mobilized nearly 65,000 troops for nuclear exercises.
The drills include over 200 missile launchers and 140 aircraft.
Belarusian forces are participating in parallel exercises.
The exercises are seen as a response to NATO and Western military actions.
Russia's nuclear arsenal is the largest in the world, with 4,400 active weapons.
The timing coincides with President Putin's diplomatic visit to China.
document.why_it_matters: The exercises highlight Russia's military capabilities and strategic intentions amidst ongoing geopolitical tensions, particularly with NATO. They serve as a demonstration of military readiness and a signal to both domestic and international audiences regarding Russia's nuclear posture.

claims.claim_text: Russia launched its largest nuclear exercises in years.
claims.evidence_text: Russia launched its largest nuclear exercises in years on Tuesday, mobilizing nearly 65,000 troops.
A new dimension of these drills is the explicit integration of Belarus.

claims.claim_text: The drills are framed as a rehearsal for nuclear responses.
claims.evidence_text: The Russian Defense Ministry announced the maneuvers... framing them as a rehearsal for 'the preparation and use of nuclear forces in the event of a threat of aggression.'

entities.name: Russia
entities.description: The country conducting the nuclear exercises.
entities.evidence_text: Russia launched its largest nuclear exercises in years.

entities.name: Belarus
entities.description: The country participating in parallel exercises with Russia.
entities.evidence_text: The Belarusian Defense Ministry announced its own parallel exercise on May 18.

entities.name: Strategic Missile Forces
entities.description: One of the military units involved in the exercises.
entities.evidence_text: The exercises involve the Strategic Missile Forces.

events.evidence_text: The Russian Defense Ministry announced the maneuvers without prior public notice on May 19.
The exercises also follow a successful Sarmat ICBM test launch on May 12.
